meta.sources: Walk Score, U.S. Census ACS 2022, Census CBP 2022, NIAAA Surveillance Report #120
meta.method: Normalized scoring from public data sources. Tourist-market bar density capped at 4.0/10K. See scrape-instructions/ for full methodology.

temporal.note: 85% commute rate with 37.1-min average travel time

spatial.note: Walk Score 100, 2.1 bars/10K, very dense urban core

planning.note: Transit Score 100 and Walk Score 100 make spontaneous outings easy

permission.note: NY ranks mid-range in per-capita consumption (2.76 gal, range 1.5–4.0)

repetition.note: Very stable population (2.9% moved in), regulars keep coming back

markets.msaCaveat: Scores reflect the full New York-Newark-Jersey City, NY-NJ-PA MSA (19,908,595 people, 6,718 sq mi). The urban core likely outperforms these numbers—suburban sprawl dilutes timing, density, and walkability metrics.
